First public release: render the Carve markup language to safe HTML in Shopware 6.6 and 6.7.

### Features

Ten surfaces, one plain-text source:

- Twig filters `|carve`, `|carve_text`, `|carve_md`
- Carve CMS element and block
- Product custom field `carve_body`
- Category custom field `carve_category_body`
- Manufacturer Carve field
- Admin live preview (carve-js, byte-identical to the storefront render)
- Transactional mail (`|carve` + `|carve_text`)
- Inline `:product[SKU]` references via `|carve_ctx(context)`
- Optional Carve rendering of product reviews (comment profile, UGC-safe)
- `carve:render` CLI (HTML / Markdown / plain / ANSI)

Plus:

- Always-on extensions: admonitions, details, list tables, footnotes, autolink, external links (`rel="nofollow noopener"` + `target="_blank"`), table of contents, and spoilers (native `<details>` block + CSS-blur inline, no JavaScript).
- Plugin settings (Admin -> Extensions -> Carve -> Configure): `allowRawHtml` (default off), `livePreview`, `smartQuotes` + `smartQuotesLocale` (20 locales), and opt-in `enableMermaid` / `enableCharts` (lazy-load from a CDN only when a diagram is on the page).
- Storefront `.carve-content` stylesheet covering all rendered constructs.

### Security

- URL-scheme denylist (`javascript:`, `vbscript:`, `data:`, `file:`) and attribute hardening (`on*`, `srcdoc`, `formaction`, script-bearing CSS) are always-on baselines from carve-php, independent of any setting. `allowRawHtml` governs only raw HTML passthrough and is off by default.
- Untrusted review text is hardened by the comment profile: bold, italic, and links survive; headings, images, and raw `<script>` degrade to inert text.

Full threat model and a Carve-vs-Markdown comparison: [`docs/security.md`](https://github.com/markup-carve/shopware-carve/blob/main/docs/security.md).

### Install

```bash
composer require markup-carve/shopware-carve
bin/console plugin:refresh
bin/console plugin:install --activate ShopwareCarve
bin/console cache:clear
```

Or upload the attached `ShopwareCarve-0.1.0.zip` via Admin -> Extensions -> My extensions -> Upload extension.

Requires PHP 8.2+ and Shopware 6.6 or 6.7.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/markup-carve/shopware-carve/commits/0.1.0
